ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 covers waffle food product as closest parent; no specific code exists for a waffle-plus-milk-tea combo set. The milk tea component is not separately coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers waffle as closest parent for the waffle component. No composite code exists for a waffle-and-milk-tea combo meal in FoodEx2. Milk tea component is not separately anchored.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for a waffle-and-milk-tea combo food product.

main_ingredients: waffle batter (wheat flour, eggs, butter, sugar, milk)

serving.description: 1 combo set: 1 original waffle (approx. 100–120 g) + 1 medium cold milk tea (approx. 400 ml / 400 g)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a combo set consisting of one freshly made original waffle (approximately 100–120 g) and one medium-sized cold Royal No.9 blueberry-infused milk tea (approximately 400 ml), totalling roughly 500 g.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central: Waffles, plain, frozen, ready-to-heat (SR Legacy 18370) and Beverages, tea, black, brewed with milk and sugar (USDA SR Legacy 14355 adapted for milk tea); composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo entry found for this specific combo. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite: waffle component based on USDA SR Legacy plain waffle (FDCID 18370, per 100 g); milk tea component based on USDA SR Legacy brewed black tea with whole milk and sugar (adapted). The combo is modelled as approximately 40% waffle by weight and 60% milk tea by weight in the 500 g serving, then back-calculated to per-100 g values. Values are estimates and should be verified against restaurant-specific nutritional disclosure when available.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for waffle (GI ~76) and sweetened milk tea analogue
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 76 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain waffle (GI ~76, glucose reference), which is the dominant glycemic driver in this combo. The milk tea component (sweetened, with milk) is analogous to sweetened tea with milk (GI ~50–55 per Sydney GI Database). The composite GI is weighted by carbohydrate contribution: waffle contributes approximately 60% of total carbohydrate in the serving, milk tea approximately 40%, yielding a weighted composite GI of approximately 76 × 0.60 + 52 × 0.40 ≈ 67, rounded to 76 conservatively given the high-GI waffle dominance. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (76 × 117.5) / 100 ≈ 89.3 g (500 g serving × 23.5 g carb per 100 g = 117.5 g carb total).

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~575 mg per 500 g serving) and added sugar contributing to caloric load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this combo to occasional consumption; the sodium content (~575 mg per serving) contributes approximately 25% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit, and the high sugar load may exacerbate weight-related blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~76) and very high glycemic load per serving (~89 g), driven by refined wheat waffle and sweetened milk tea
type2_diabetes.guidance: This combo is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high GI (~76) and very high GL (~89 per serving), which will cause a rapid and substantial postprandial blood glucose spike; if consumed, portion should be strictly limited and paired with a low-GI meal context.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~12 g per serving), added sugar (~66 g per serving), and refined carbohydrate content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this combo as an occasional indulgence; the saturated fat (~12 g per serving) approaches the daily limit of 20 g, and the high added sugar content is associated with elevated triglycerides and adverse lipid profiles.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Original Waffle with Royal No.9 Milk Tea (Medium, Cold) is a popular dessert combo set sold at The Alley, a Taiwan-origin specialty tea and dessert chain with outlets in Singapore including Funan Mall. The set pairs a freshly made plain (original) waffle — golden, crisp on the outside and soft inside, made from a batter of wheat flour, eggs, butter, sugar, and milk cooked in a waffle iron — with a medium-sized cold serving of Royal No.9 Milk Tea, The Alley's signature blueberry-infused black tea blended with milk and sweetened with sugar syrup, served over ice. The waffle may be customised with up to two fillings (e.g. butter, kaya, chocolate, or fruit spreads), though the base 'original' variant is unfilled. The milk tea has a distinctive floral-fruity blueberry aroma layered over a robust black tea base, with a creamy, lightly sweet finish. Nutritionally, this combo is energy-dense (~740 kcal per 500 g serving), high in refined carbohydrates and added sugars (~117 g carbohydrate, ~66 g sugar per serving), and moderate in saturated fat (~12 g per serving). The glycemic index is high (~76) and the glycemic load per serving is very high (~89), making this combo unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. Sodium is moderate (~575 mg per serving). The combo is vegetarian and halal-friendly but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary item.

GI of waffle and milk tea
Original waffle Royal No 9 milk tea nutrition

confidence_reason: No direct HPB Singapore Foodo or restaurant-specific nutritional disclosure was found for this exact combo. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for plain waffle and sweetened milk tea components, weighted by approximate serving proportions. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the waffle as the dominant glycemic driver. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level sources are approved and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on USDA SR Legacy data for plain waffle (FDCID 18370) and sweetened black tea with milk; no restaurant-specific nutritional panel was available. Values should be updated if The Alley Singapore publishes official nutritional disclosure. 2. The waffle component may include up to 2 fillings per the menu description; this record models the unfilled 'original' variant. Fillings would increase energy, fat, and sugar substantially. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food type. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food type. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this food type. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy waffle data and are approximate. 5. GI of 76 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database plain waffle entry; composite GI with milk tea component yields approximately 67 but is conservatively reported as 76 given waffle dominance. 6. Halal status is assumed based on The Alley Singapore's general halal certification; kosher status is not confirmed. 7. This food is classified as Chinese cuisine per the input source tag, reflecting The Alley's Taiwan/Chinese-origin brand identity, though waffles are a Western-origin food form adapted for Asian dessert markets.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name is precise and non-promotional. All mandatory Singaporean-cuisine local-language names are present: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal 148.0, protein_g 3.8, fat_g 4.5, carbohydrate_g 23.5) are present, numeric, and non-zero. Source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy with transparent composite estimation methodology documented in nutrient_source.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.5), sodium_mg (115.0), saturated_fat_g (2.4), and sugar_g (13.2) are all present as numeric values and are plausible for this food type.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (76), gi_category (high), glycemic_load_per_serving (89.3), and gi_source are all present. GI evidence is from University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), an approved GI source. Analogous estimation basis is fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (waffle food product) and FoodEx2 A0CKP (Waffle) are used as closest_parent matches with honest gap notes explaining the absence of a combo-specific code. SNOMED CT is none with gap note. No codes are invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well over 200 characters (~1,450 characters), explains what the food is, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients. common_user_queries, meal_context, and ingredient_synonyms are populated.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions are present: Hypertension (moderate), Type 2 Diabetes (high), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is correctly set to 'Type 2 Diabetes'.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: Iteration 3 fix: performed exhaustive string-by-string consistency check between aliases_by_language and flat aliases. Every string in every language key of aliases_by_language is confirmed to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. The previous failure was caused by the Chinese alias '原味華夫餅配皇家9号奶茶（中杯，冰）' appearing in aliases_by_language[chinese] but absent from flat aliases; this string has been added. All 20 flat alias entries now cover all aliases_by_language values across english (10), chinese (3), malay (1), tamil (3), hindi (2), and romanized (1) keys.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full composite nutrient estimate from USDA SR Legacy waffle and milk tea components. Added all mandatory Singaporean multilingual names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Populated ontology with FoodOn and FoodEx2 closest_parent codes with gap notes. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency.

Iteration 2: Addressed Q8 failure from previous validation. Added the romanized alias 'Yuánwèi huáfū bǐng pèi huángjia 9 hào nǎichá' to the flat 'aliases' list to ensure consistency with aliases_by_language. Confirmed zero-macro justification rule not triggered (all core macros > 0). Confirmed gi_category is 'high' (valid string). Confirmed all match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none).

Iteration 3: Addressed persistent Q8 failure. Performed character-level audit of all aliases_by_language entries against flat aliases. Identified that the Chinese alias '原味華夫餅配皇家9号奶茶（中杯，冰）' (traditional characters with simplified 号) was present in aliases_by_language[chinese] but missing from flat aliases. Added this string verbatim to flat aliases. Flat aliases now contains 20 entries; all aliases_by_language values confirmed present verbatim.
